topic: Someone discovers their partner has been writing a novel about them
raw_output:
[story 1]

The discovery happened while I was tidying the study. A stack of loose papers, tucked beneath a copy of *Wuthering Heights*, had slipped out. My breath hitched when I saw the title page. It wasn't a grocery list or a work document; it was a manuscript. The first few lines were undeniably about me—my morning coffee ritual, the way I frowned when concentrating, the specific shade of blue in my eyes. A cold, heavy sensation settled in my chest, making my hands tremble slightly as I read further. I backed away, the sound of my own ragged breathing loud in the sudden silence. When my partner walked in, smiling that easy, familiar smile, I couldn't meet their gaze. I just pointed a stiff finger at the papers, my throat tight. "What is this?" The air in the room felt thick, suffocating.

[story 2]

I found the evidence tucked inside an old leather-bound journal, hidden beneath a pile of forgotten letters. It was a sprawling narrative, detailed and intensely personal, charting my moods, my small victories, the mundane choreography of our shared life, all woven into the fabric of a fictional story. My stomach clenched, a tight knot forming just beneath my ribs. I spent the next hour pacing the length of the living room, the floorboards cool beneath my bare feet. Every time I glanced toward the bedroom, a wave of unwelcome scrutiny washed over me. When he finally arrived, I kept my voice unnaturally level. "I found something," I managed, my eyes fixed on the rug. He stopped, his usual easy demeanor dissolving into a careful stillness. The silence stretched, taut and brittle, as he slowly approached the discovery.

[story 3]

It was the late afternoon, and I was searching for a misplaced remote control when I stumbled upon it—a collection of typed pages detailing my deepest insecurities and most fleeting joys, framed as chapters in a novel. My vision blurred momentarily. My hands, which had been steady moments before, began to shake uncontrollably. I dropped the papers, the sound echoing sharply in the quiet apartment. I felt a profound sense of exposure, as if every private corner of my existence had been mapped and cataloged by someone else. When my partner entered, I didn't speak. I simply stood by the doorway, my posture rigid, my jaw clenched so tightly I could feel the strain in my temples. The look he gave me—a mixture of surprise and something guarded—told me everything I needed to know about the gravity of the situation.
